title: LVD Lab Transcardial Perfusion Protocol
author: Laura Volpicelli-Daley
materials: 2. Make sure a thermometer is placed in dry ice/2-methylbutane mixture. Ensure the temperature stays between -20 to -30°C for snap-freezing brain tissue.

4. Use forceps to carefully place the brain into the 2-methylbutane (kept between -20 to -30°C with dry ice), which should be in a plastic beaker. Temperature of the 2-methylbutane should be monitored the entire time with a thermometer.

steps.title: 1X PBS Contributed by users with 0
steps.instruction: 1X PBS Contributed by users with 0.005 % (v/v) Sodium nitroprusside Contributed by users and 10 U/mL Heparin sodium Contributed by users ( 1 L ) 990 mL 1X PBS Contributed by users 1X PBS Contributed by users 0.05 g Sodium nitroprusside Contributed by users Catalog #A15656 (RRID: sodium pentacyanonitrosylferra) 10 mL Heparin sodium Contributed by users Catalog #25021-400-30

steps.title: Cryoprotectant Solution
steps.instruction: Cryoprotectant Solution: 30 % Sucrose/PBS 30 g 30% Sucrose Contributed by users per 100 mL 1X PBS Contributed by users (final volume) E.g., 500 mL 30% Sucrose Contributed by users = 150 g sucrose dissolved in 1X PBS Contributed by users then filled to a total of 500 mL solution.

steps.title: Fixative
steps.instruction: Fixative: 4% Paraformaldehyde Contributed by users /PBS* (See notes to determine volume necessary) (1L)

steps.title: Measure Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Thermo Scientific Catalog...
steps.instruction: Measure Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Thermo Scientific Catalog #AAA113130E out in fume hood so that final concentration will be 4 % (v/v) (e.g., for one liter, 40 g of Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Thermo Scientific Catalog #AAA113130E is added to ddH₂O Contributed by users ).

steps.title: Add Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Thermo Scientific Catalog...
steps.instruction: Add Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Thermo Scientific Catalog #AAA113130E to the hot water with stir bar stirring vigorously and wait for the majority of the Paraformaldehyde (PFA) Thermo Scientific Catalog #AAA113130E to go into solution. - While waiting for PFA to dissolve, create an ice bath that will accommodate your beaker with ice and water for step 3.5.

steps.title: Dropwise, add concentrated NaOH Fisher Scientific...
steps.instruction: Dropwise, add concentrated NaOH Fisher Scientific Catalog #SS267 to the solution so that it is no longer cloudy. Small bits floating about are ok.

steps.title: Move the beaker to your ice...
steps.instruction: Move the beaker to your ice bath and monitor temperature until it reaches 25 °C, (20-25°C) .

steps.title: Add 10X PBS Contributed by users...
steps.instruction: Add 10X PBS Contributed by users to a final concentration of 1X (e.g., for one 1 L final volume, 100 mL of 10X PBS Contributed by users is added).

steps.title: Add ddH₂O Contributed by users to...
steps.instruction: Add ddH₂O Contributed by users to final volume marked on beaker ( 1000 mL ).

steps.title: Adjust the pH of the solution to pH 7
steps.instruction: Adjust the pH of the solution to pH 7.4 with NaOH Fisher Scientific Catalog #SS267 or HCl Contributed by users .

steps.title: Filter the solution through 0
steps.instruction: Filter the solution through 0.2 μm or 0.45 μm filter flask.

steps.title: Solution can be stored at 4...
steps.instruction: Solution can be stored at 4 °C for up to one week or aliquoted to 50 mL tubes and stored in -20 °C for up to 6 months.

steps.title: Anesthetize mouse via isoflurane Contributed by...
steps.instruction: Anesthetize mouse via isoflurane Contributed by users inhalation described in approved IACUC protocol until mouse no longer responds to tail or toe pinch. Work as quickly as possible after this step.

steps.title: Transcardially perfuse mouse with cold 1X...
steps.instruction: Transcardially perfuse mouse with cold 1X PBS Contributed by users containing Heparin sodium Contributed by users Catalog #25021-400-30 (keep on ice) → 25 mL total per mouse at 3 ml/min (Setting “8” on pump). After you cut the diaphragm, the mouse is no longer alive, but its heart will continue to beat. The sooner you place the needle, the more successful your perfusion will be.

steps.title: If 4% PFA Contributed by users perfusion is needed
steps.instruction: If 4% PFA Contributed by users perfusion is needed: Perfuse mouse with cold 4% PFA Contributed by users in 1X PBS Contributed by users (keep on ice) → 25 mL total per mouse at 9 ml/min (Setting “8” on pump). a. Successful circulation of PFA throughout the mouse will often cause the mouse’s tail to move, flick, or stiffen during the perfusion. b. If perfusing multiple mice in a row, ensure to clear the tubing of excess PFA before beginning the next mouse by running a sufficient volume of 1X PBS solution through the tubing and allowing the PFA to collect in a separate waste container.

steps.title: For histological analyses
steps.instruction: For histological analyses: Dissect out desired tissue samples and post-fix in 4% PFA Contributed by users in 1X PBS Contributed by users for 24:00:00 at 4 °C (see Note 11).

steps.title: Brain
steps.instruction: Brain: After post-fixing in 4% PFA Contributed by users in 1X PBS Contributed by users for 24:00:00 , rinse tissue with 1X PBS Contributed by users (no sodium nitroprusside/heparin) then place brain tissue in 30% Sucrose Contributed by users in PBS solution until the brain sinks to the bottom of the glass vial (est. 48:00:00, (24 to 48 hours) ). After the brain sinks, snap-freeze the tissue in 2-methylbutane Contributed by users and dry ice (See Note 12) until the brain stops bubbling (est. 00:01:00, (30 to 60 sec) ). Store the frozen brains individually wrapped in labeled, aluminum foil, placed in a cardboard box at 80°C. For convenience and clear identification, it is recommended to use solvent and temperature resistant printable labels to label each brain wrapped in foil (Tough-Tags Labels on Sheets, 9187-2816).

1. After placing tissue samples in 4% PFA for post-fixing, immediately place glass vials on ice before storing at 4°C for the 24-hour post-fixing period. Over-fixation of tissues can lead to serious technical difficulties and experimental complications downstream, strict adherence to time and temperature guidelines when tissue is in fixative is vital.

3. After post-fixing tissue in 4% PFA for 24 hours, rinse with 1X PBS (no sodium nitroprusside or heparin). For cryostat or microtome sectioning, place the 1X PBS-rinsed brains in 30% sucrose until the brain sinks then snap-freeze. For paraffin-embedding, place 1X PBS-rinsed tissue in 70% ethanol until paraffin embedding.
5. Assume you will need ~35 mL of 4% PFA per mouse (including post-fix + room for error). Final volume (mL) = # of mice * 35 mL + 100

1. Using an accurate glass beaker or a beaker marked with the correct final volume as measured with an accurate cylinder, add ~80% (~ 800 mL ) of the desired final volume of ddH₂O Contributed by users to the beaker, add a stir bar and place the beaker on a hot plate (inside fume hood) set to reach around ~ 60 °C . a. As the temperature approaches -60 °C, (55-60°C) , turn off the hot plate, the temperature does not need to be kept at 60 °C . b. If the temperature goes above 60 °C , wait for it to cool before proceeding. PFA powder is especially toxic, care should be taken to not breathe it in while measuring and solubilizing, perform as many steps as possible in a fume hood with appropriate PPE. Making PFA can be time consuming, preparing all of the reagents the day before is recommended.

steps.title: Place the needle through the apex...
steps.instruction: Place the needle through the apex of the heart and keep the needle as straight and to the left (the mouse’s left) as possible to remain in the left ventricle and avoid crossing the heart’s septum.

steps.title: After the needle is placed, cut...
steps.instruction: After the needle is placed, cut the right atrium with scissors, turn on the pump, and manually hold the needle in place until the animal has been perfused with 25 mL of 1X PBS Contributed by users . A sign of a good perfusion is when the liver clears of blood and turns to a lighter color. If you see liquid drip from the mouse’s nose or mouth, it may indicate the needle was placed too deep in the heart – this doesn’t always result in a poor perfusion, but is something to note if unexpected results occur in downstream experiments.
